title: Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Chitradurga district of Karnataka.
overview.description: Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Chitradurga district of Karnataka. The wooded region of Jogimatti is a hill station and is rich in biodiversity. The area is picturesque, and is relatively compact with a wide range of flora and fauna. It is notified through an official notification on 23 December 2015.

topPlaces.name: Jogimatti
topPlaces.description: Jogimatti is a hill station and forest reserve in Chitradurga district, Karnataka, India. The reserve covers 10,048.97 hectares (38.7993 sq mi) in Chitradurga, Holalkere and Hiriyur taluks, 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) south of the city of Chitradurga.

topPlaces.name: Chandravalli
topPlaces.description: Chandravalli is an archaeological site located in the Chitradurga district of the state of Karnataka, India. The region is a valley formed by three hills, Chitradurga, Kirabanakallu and Jolagudda.

topPlaces.name: Bharamasagara
topPlaces.description: Bharamasagara is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Chitradurga taluk of Chitradurga district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Turuvanur
topPlaces.description: Turuvanur is a village in Chitradurga taluk in the south Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.description: Doddasiddavvanahalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Chitradurga taluk of Chitradurga district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Matadakurubarahatti
topPlaces.description: Matadakurubarahatti is a census town in Chitradurga district in the south Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Chitradurga
topPlaces.description: Chitradurga is a city and the headquarters of Chitradurga district, which is located on the valley of the Vedavati river in the central part of the Indian state of Karnataka. Chitradurga is a place with historical significance and a major tourist hub of Karnataka.

Chandravalli is an archaeological site located in the Chitradurga district of the state of Karnataka, India. The region is a valley formed by three hills, Chitradurga, Kirabanakallu and Jolagudda.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (123 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (183 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jogimatti, Chandravalli, Bharamasagara, Turuvanur, Doddasiddavvanahalli.
